mainEntity.name: Can I register a Mumbai company with a Thane address?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — Thane falls under ROC Mumbai jurisdiction. A Thane registered office is processed by the same ROC as Nariman Point or BKC. Significant cost savings on commercial rent.

mainEntity.name: Is Thane suitable for manufacturing company registration?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — Thane and Navi Mumbai have industrial zones well-suited for manufacturing. MIDC Thane provides plots. Virtual Auditor handles all factory-related registrations and compliance.

mainEntity.name: What is the cost advantage of Thane over South Mumbai?

acceptedAnswer.text: Commercial rent in Thane is 40-60% lower than Lower Parel/BKC. Government fees for registration are identical. The cost difference is entirely in office rent and overheads.

mainEntity.name: What company secretary services does Virtual Auditor provide in Thane, Mumbai?

acceptedAnswer.text: Complete secretarial compliance: board meetings, ROC filings (AOC-4, MGT-7A, ADT-1, DIR-3 KYC), statutory registers, ESOP administration, Section 188 RPT compliance, secretarial audit (MR-3), and corporate governance advisory. ACS-led practice. Contact +91-99622 60333.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for late ROC filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: ₹100/day per form with no cap. AOC-4 filed 1 year late = ₹36,500. Two forms × 3 years = ₹4.38 lakh. DIR-3 KYC missed = DIN deactivation (₹5,000 to reactivate). 3+ years non-filing = director disqualification from ALL boards for 5 years.

mainEntity.name: How much does a company secretary retainer cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Startup/small company: ₹36,000-₹60,000/year. Growth company with FDI/ESOP: ₹60,000-₹1,50,000/year. Complex structure: ₹1,50,000-₹5,00,000/year. The monthly retainer prevents ₹73,000+/year in late filing fees alone.

mainEntity.name: Can you remediate past filing defaults for companies in Thane?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — Virtual Auditor files late forms (with additional fees), reactivates deactivated DINs, clears pending compliance, and resolves strike-off proceedings. Remediation cost: ₹25,000-₹1,00,000 depending on backlog.

mainEntity.name: What board resolutions does my company need?

acceptedAnswer.text: 25+ resolutions from incorporation through Series B: share allotment (with FEMA language for FDI), ESOP grants, RPT approvals, auditor appointment, bank signatory, annual accounts approval, and more. Virtual Auditor drafts resolutions with FEMA, valuation, and tax cross-references.

mainEntity.name: Do you handle secretarial audit (Form MR-3)?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — Section 204 secretarial audit covering Companies Act, Secretarial Standards, FEMA, and SEBI compliance. Mandatory for listed and large public companies. Voluntary but valuable for pre-fundraise and pre-IPO companies.

mainEntity.name: Why combine CS and CA in one firm?

acceptedAnswer.text: Share allotments need board resolutions (CS) + valuations (CA) + FEMA filings (CA). RPTs need Section 188 approval (CS) + arm's length pricing (CA). One firm eliminates coordination gaps and ensures consistency across regulators. Virtual Auditor's principal holds both FCA and ACS.

acceptedAnswer.text: Company Secretary Services in Thane costs ₹36,000 – ₹5,00,000/year. Stamp duty: ₹5,000-₹6,000 (GRAS portal e-stamping). Contact Virtual Auditor at +91-99622 60333 for a specific quote.

mainEntity.name: Who is the best company secretary services provider near Thane?

acceptedAnswer.text: Virtual Auditor, led by CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333), provides company secretary services from offices in Chennai, Bangalore, and Mumbai. Website: virtualauditor.in.

acceptedAnswer.text: Requirements vary by service type. Contact Virtual Auditor at +91-99622 60333 for a specific document checklist for company secretary services in Thane.

@graph.name: How to Get Company Secretary Services in Thane, Mumbai

step.name: Compliance Assessment
step.text: Review your company's current secretarial compliance status — pending ROC filings, missing board resolutions, statutory register gaps, and AGM/meeting compliance. Identify RED (urgent), AMBER (fix within quarter), and GREEN (compliant) items.

step.name: Board Meeting Setup and Management
step.text: Schedule and manage board meetings (minimum 4/year, gap ≤120 days). Prepare agenda, circulate notice (7 days), ensure quorum, draft resolutions with FEMA/regulatory language where applicable, and record minutes within 15 days.

step.name: Annual ROC Filings
step.text: File AOC-4 (financial statements, 30 days from AGM), MGT-7A (annual return, 60 days from AGM), ADT-1 (auditor appointment), DIR-3 KYC (all directors, September 30). Late filing: ₹100/day per form with no cap.

step.name: Statutory Register Maintenance
step.text: Maintain and update: Register of Members (within 7 days of every allotment/transfer), Register of Directors and KMP, Register of Charges, Register of Contracts with Related Parties (Section 189), and Minutes Books (board + general meetings).

step.name: Event-Based Filings
step.text: File within prescribed timelines: PAS-3 (share allotment, 15 days), DIR-12 (director appointment/resignation, 30 days), SH-7 (capital alteration, 30 days), CHG-1 (charge creation, 30 days), MGT-14 (special resolutions, 30 days).

step.name: Governance Advisory and Compliance Calendar
step.text: Maintain a client-specific compliance calendar with automated reminders 30, 15, and 7 days before each deadline. Advise on board composition, committee structure, independent director requirements, vigil mechanism, and Section 188 RPT compliance.
